name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Dna Repair Enhancement bundle
summary: The taxonomy mapping is coherent. SIRT6-mediated DSB-repair efficiency, OGG1 oxidative-base-damage repair activation, and broad repair-pathway targeting context belong in DNA Repair Enhancement, while NAD/NMN/NR searches, ataxia registries, and adjacent disease-context records remain boundary or no-result material rather than public support for this track.

findings.claim_or_issue: The added SIRT6 and OGG1 anchors directly concern DNA double-strand break repair or oxidative DNA damage repair and are appropriately mapped to DNA Repair Enhancement.
findings.why_it_matters: They repair mechanistic coverage gaps without requiring a new intervention family or adjacent-track reassignment.

findings.claim_or_issue: NAD/NMN/NR material, ataxia registry checks, and broader oncology or neurodegeneration DNA repair targeting context remain excluded or boundary context rather than public evidence support.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents the track from absorbing generic redox, disease-registry, or cancer-sensitization claims that do not test aging-directed DNA repair enhancement.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; revisit only if future sources produce direct aging-relevant repair-enhancement evidence.
